q: Which country will host the 20th Asian Games in 2026?
explanation: The 20th Asian Games (19 Sep-4 Oct 2026) will be hosted by Aichi-Nagoya, Japan — the third time Japan hosts the Asiad (Tokyo 1958, Hiroshima 1994). China hosted the 19th edition (Hangzhou 2023).

q: At the Paris 2024 Olympics, which of the following games was NOT included?
explanation: Cricket was last played at the 1900 Paris Olympics and returns only at LA28 (T20, 6 teams per gender, Fairplex, Pomona — confirmed April 2025). Fencing, Modern Pentathlon and Triathlon are core Olympic sports.

q: Match the Grand Slam with its venue: a) Australian Open b) French Open c) Wimbledon d) US Open — with 1) All England Club 2) Melbourne Park 3) Roland Garros 4) Flushing Meadows
explanation: Australian Open-Melbourne Park; French Open-Roland Garros; Wimbledon-All England Club; US Open-Flushing Meadows. The 69th BPSC trap paired Flushing Meadows with the French Open.

q: The art of crafting which item in Meerut has received a GI tag?
explanation: Meerut Scissors is the registered GI — 'Meerut Scissors' holds GI Registration Application No. 389, registered by the GI Registry in April 2015. Meerut's celebrated sports goods are the district's ODOP (One District One Product) product, NOT a registered GI, so option (A) is technically incorrect. Expert keys for this real 71st BPSC question are split: Drishti/Genius key it as Scissors, while StudyIQ (claiming to mirror the official key) says Sports Goods — recognize this question if it is repeated. Correct Answer: (D)

q: Who is the author of the autobiography 'I Have the Streets: A Kutti Cricket Story'?
explanation: Off-spinner R. Ashwin co-wrote the memoir with journalist Sidharth Monga. Ashwin retired from all international cricket on 18 December 2024.

q: Shreyasi Singh, MLA, who represented India in shooting at the Paris Olympics 2024, was elected from which constituency in Bihar?
explanation: Shreyasi Singh, an international shooter and Paris 2024 Olympian, is the BJP MLA from Jamui — a signature Bihar polity x sports crossover.

q: Which field game is the most popular in the world?
explanation: Football (soccer) is the world's most popular field game by following and participation — fitting with the 48-team FIFA World Cup 2026 in USA/Canada/Mexico.

q: Which country hosted the 2022 Winter Olympics, and which hosts the 2026 edition?
explanation: Beijing (China) hosted the 2022 Winter Olympics; Milano-Cortina (Italy) hosted the 2026 edition (6-22 Feb 2026; mascots Milo & Tina). Next: French Alps 2030.

q: At the Paris 2024 Olympics, who became the first Indian since Independence to win two medals at a single Olympic Games?
explanation: Manu Bhaker won bronze in the 10m air pistol AND the mixed team event (with Sarabjot Singh) — first Indian post-1947 with two medals at one Games. India's Paris total: 6 medals (1S, 5B).

q: Match the trophy with its sport: a) Durand Cup b) Ranji Trophy c) Thomas Cup d) Beighton Cup — with 1) Cricket 2) Football 3) Badminton 4) Hockey
explanation: Durand Cup (1888, world's 3rd-oldest football tournament)-Football; Ranji Trophy (1934-35)-Cricket; Thomas Cup-Badminton; Beighton Cup-Hockey.

q: Statements about ICC Champions Trophy 2025: 1. India defeated New Zealand by 4 wickets in the final. 2. The final was played at Dubai. 3. It was India's third Champions Trophy title. Which are correct?
explanation: All three: final on 9 Mar 2025 at Dubai (Rohit Sharma 76, POTM); India's CT titles: 2002 (shared), 2013, 2025. New Zealand lost both ICC white-ball finals to India in this window.

q: The ICC World Test Championship Final 2025 at Lord's was won by:
options: Australia, beating South Africa by 5 wickets
South Africa, beating Australia by 5 wickets
England, beating Australia at The Oval
explanation: South Africa chased 282 (283/4) to beat Australia by 5 wickets (11-14 Jun 2025) — their FIRST WTC title; Aiden Markram's 136 was Player of the Match.

q: The ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 final was decided by which result?
options: India beat New Zealand by 96 runs at Narendra Modi Stadium, Ahmedabad
India beat England by 7 runs at Wankhede Stadium, Mumbai
South Africa beat India by 76 runs in Ahmedabad
New Zealand beat India in Colombo
explanation: Final: 8 Mar 2026, Ahmedabad — India d. New Zealand by 96 runs; Bumrah 4/15 (POTM). Option (B) was the semi-final; (C) was India's only loss (Super 8). India = 3rd title, first back-to-back winner, first host winner.

q: India's ICC Women's ODI World Cup 2025 triumph is best described as:
options: India's third women's World Cup title, won against Australia
A title won on English soil at Lord's
India's MAIDEN women's ODI World Cup, beating South Africa by 52 runs at DY Patil, Navi Mumbai
A shared title after a rain-washed final
explanation: 2 Nov 2025: India 298/7 (Shafali Verma 87 + 2/36) beat South Africa 246 — maiden title. Deepti Sharma POT (22 wickets incl. 5/39 in the final). Harmanpreet = 3rd Indian WC-winning captain.

q: The ICC U19 World Cup 2026 final (Harare) saw India:
options: Win a record 6th title, beating England by 100 runs, powered by Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's 175
Lose to England despite Sooryavanshi's century
Win on reserve day after rain in Bulawayo
explanation: 6 Feb 2026, Harare: IND 411/9 (Sooryavanshi 175 off 80 — highest in a U19 WC final) beat England 311. Sooryavanshi (Samastipur, Bihar) was POTM + Player of the Tournament (439 runs). India's 6th U19 crown is the most by any nation.

q: Which statement correctly captures IPL 2025 and IPL 2026?
options: RCB won their maiden title in 2025 (d. Punjab Kings) and defended it in 2026 (d. Gujarat Titans) — both finals at Ahmedabad
Chennai Super Kings won both editions
Gujarat Titans won 2025 and Royal Challengers Bengaluru won 2026
Mumbai Indians ended their title drought in 2026
explanation: IPL 2025: RCB d. PBKS by 6 runs (3 Jun 2025); IPL 2026: RCB d. GT by 5 wickets (31 May 2026; Kohli 75* POTM). Sooryavanshi swept IPL 2026 Orange Cap + MVP + Emerging Player; Purple Cap: Rabada (29).

q: Statements about Khelo India Youth Games 2025: 1. Hosted by Bihar for the first time. 2. Mascot was Gajasimha (Pala-era/Nalanda motif). 3. Bihar finished 15th with 36 medals — best-ever. Which are correct?
explanation: All three: 7th KIYG (4-15 May 2025) in Patna, Rajgir, Gaya, Bhagalpur, Begusarai (+ New Delhi); Maharashtra topped (158 medals); Bihar's 36 medals (7G-11S-18B) doubled its all-time KIYG tally.

q: The final of the Men's Hockey Asia Cup 2025 — won by India — was played at:
explanation: Rajgir, Bihar hosted the 12th Men's Asia Cup (29 Aug-7 Sep 2025); India beat Korea 4-1 for a 4th title and FIH WC 2026 qualification. Pakistan withdrew; Malaysia finished 3rd.

q: Regarding the National Games of India, pick the correct statement:
options: Maharashtra topped the 38th National Games held in Goa
The 39th National Games (2027) will be hosted by Uttarakhand
Services (SSCB) topped the 38th National Games (Uttarakhand, 2025) to lift the Raja Bhalindra Singh Trophy; Meghalaya hosts the 39th edition in 2027
The 2025 edition was the 40th National Games
explanation: 38th National Games: Uttarakhand, 28 Jan-14 Feb 2025; Services 121 medals (68 gold) — Raja Bhalindra Singh Trophy; mascot Mauli, torch Tejaswini. Meghalaya hosts the 39th (2027).

q: At the Sepak Takraw World Cup 2025 held in Patna, India achieved which milestone?
options: The men's regu team won its first-ever World Cup gold, defeating Japan 2-1
India topped the medal tally with 21 golds
India won silver in all team events
The event marked sepak takraw's removal from the Asian Games roster
explanation: 5th ISTAF World Cup, Patliputra Indoor Stadium, Patna (20-25 Mar 2025), 21 nations: India men's regu beat Japan 2-1 for a historic first gold; India won 7 medals (4th in tally).

q: Statements about Australian Open 2026 men's singles: 1. Alcaraz defeated Djokovic in the final. 2. Alcaraz became the youngest man in the Open Era to complete the career Grand Slam. 3. He is the 9th man overall to complete a career slam. Which are correct?
explanation: All three: Alcaraz d. Djokovic 2-6 6-2 6-3 7-5 for maiden AO (7th slam); youngest career-slammer of the Open Era at 22; 9th man overall. Trap: he did NOT win Roland Garros 2026 (wrist injury — Zverev won).

q: Statements about Wimbledon 2026: 1. Sinner defeated Zverev to defend his men's title. 2. Noskova won the women's title in an all-Czech final. 3. Swiatek defended the women's singles title. Which are correct?
explanation: 1 and 2 correct: Sinner d. Zverev in 4 sets (back-to-back); Noskova d. Muchova — youngest Wimbledon women's champion in 15 years. Trap: Swiatek won in 2025 but did not defend in 2026.

q: Statements about chess 2025-26: 1. Sindarov won the 2026 Candidates and will challenge Gukesh. 2. Divya Deshmukh won the 2025 FIDE Women's World Cup, beating Koneru Humpy in an all-Indian final. 3. Praggnanandhaa is the reigning classical World Champion. Which are correct?
explanation: 1 and 2 correct (Sindarov: record 10/14 in Cyprus; Divya became India's 88th GM at Batumi). Trap: GUKESH is champion (d. Ding Liren, Dec 2024); Pragg won Tata Steel Masters 2025.

q: What precisely happened when Neeraj Chopra threw 90.23m at the Doha Diamond League (16 May 2025)?
options: He became the first Indian to cross 90m (national record) but finished second behind Germany's Julian Weber (91.06m)
He won gold with a world-leading throw
He crossed 90m at the Tokyo World Championships
He broke the Olympic record set in Tokyo 2020
explanation: The 90m barrier fell at Doha, yet it was SILVER — Weber's 91.06m won. At the Tokyo Worlds (Sep 2025) Neeraj was 8th and India won zero medals (gold: Keshorn Walcott, TTO).

q: Statements about Glasgow Commonwealth Games 2026: 1. Only 10 sports will feature. 2. Hockey, cricket, wrestling and badminton are among the dropped sports. 3. The Games run 23 July to 2 August 2026. Which are correct?
explanation: All three: 23rd CWG is a slimmed-down edition (~3,000 athletes, 74 nations, 10 sports); shooting, squash and TT also dropped. Opens 23 Jul 2026 — three days before the BPSC paper. India's 2030 CWG bid (Ahmedabad) is at proposal stage.
